game_name: Street Fighter II: The World Warrior

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Speed vs. strength tradeoff across three punch buttons
Speed vs. strength tradeoff across three kick buttons
Location-based stage selection affects opponent and environment
Learning counter-strategies for each of 8+ opponent types

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Short, intense rounds (timed one-on-one matches) with rhythm determined by player skill and opponent AI aggression; tension builds through health depletion and timer pressure, releases at round/match conclusion; bonus stages provide brief mechanical respite

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponents with basic attack patterns; players learn fundamental controls and single-button attacks
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mid-tier opponents introduce special moves and combo patterns; world map navigation adds strategic opponent selection; bonus stages provide point farming opportunities
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss-tier opponents (Sagat, Vega, M. Bison) feature aggressive AI, advanced special moves, and punishing combo patterns; requires mastery of character-specific techniques and defensive play
notable_spikes: Boss fight difficulty jump (final opponents significantly more aggressive)
Multiplayer mode difficulty spike when transitioning from AI to human opponents

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode presents sequential opponent matches with world map stage selection between encounters. After every third match, bonus stage appears for optional point accumulation. Two-round victory requirement per match maintains escalating tension.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Match progression: Ryu vs. E. Honda → world map → Ryu vs. Blanka → bonus stage → Ryu vs. Guile

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 8 playable characters features unique fighting style, special move set, and speed/strength profile. Character selection fundamentally shapes player experience and difficulty curve. Opponent AI varies significantly by character archetype.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Playing as Zangief (grappler) vs. Dhalsim (zoner) creates entirely different match pacing and strategy against same opponent

notable_level_patterns.description: Destructible-object minigames appear every third match, providing mechanical variety break and point-earning opportunity without combat pressure. Reduces player fatigue in extended play sessions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bonus stage: smash car, wooden barrels, or metal oil drums within time limit
